# UC-17.3.67 · Forcepoint Web Security On-Premises URL Blocks (`websense:cg:kv`)

## Description

Traditional Forcepoint Web Security appliances still enforce acceptable-use and threat categories for many enterprises. Blocked URL telemetry validates policy strictness, identifies compromised accounts generating unusual categories, and supports HR or insider investigations.

## Value

Maintains parity between legacy on-prem web proxies and newer SSE dashboards inside a single Splunk search head.

## Implementation

Forward appliance syslog to Splunk with the vendor-recommended KV format. Install Splunkbase 2966 for CIM Web mappings. Map `category` to internal risk tiers. Deprecation planning: migrate searches to Forcepoint ONE (`forcepoint:one`) as appliances are replaced.

## SPL

```spl
index=proxy sourcetype="websense:cg:kv" earliest=-7d
| eval act=lower(coalesce(action, disposition, result))
| where match(act,"(?i)block|deny|drop")
| stats count by user, url, category, policy
| sort -count
| head 40
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
  by All_Traffic.user, All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest span=1h
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (user, URL category), Bar chart (blocks by category), Timeline.

- [Splunk Documentation — Source types for Splunk Add-on for Forcepoint Web Security](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/AddOns/released/WebsenseCG/Sourcetypes)
